regime_justification: USDJPY plunged ~3% in suspected MOF intervention May 1 after breaking above 160; yen strengthening sharply, JGB yields at 1997 highs, and Nikkei under pressure despite modest gains—CARRY-OFF conditions active.

domino_stage.label: Shock / Trigger
domino_stage.justification: MOF intervened May 1 after USDJPY breached 160 (Katayama issued 'final warning' morning of May 1); yen surged >3% intraday to 155.5, BOJ data suggests ~$35B spent—sharp JPY move and vol spike define Stage 3.

1w.label: Likely
1w.driver: MOF already fired; forced unwind signatures emerging—watch for follow-on intervention, USDJPY defense of 155-157 zone, and cross-asset vol spillover.

2w.label: Likely
2w.driver: BOJ next meets June 15-16; markets pricing further intervention risk and carry deleveraging—vol remains elevated, spread compression continues if yen holds gains.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Unwind momentum may ease if USDJPY stabilizes and oil pulls back; BOJ meeting window ahead introduces policy-hike risk, sustaining fragility but not guaranteed escalation.

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: If intervention holds yen below 160 and Iran ceasefire progresses, carry may partially rebuild; however, BOJ hike probability and JGB stress keep tail risk live.

bottom_line: Stage 3 trigger fired: MOF intervened May 1 (~$35B, unconfirmed) after USDJPY hit 160.66, driving yen to 155.5 intraday and closing ~157. BOJ held rates at 0.75% Apr 28 but raised inflation forecast and warned on yen pass-through; three board members dissented for hike. JGB 10Y at 2.51% (1997 high), MOVE 70.4, VIX 16.99, WTI $101-105 (Iran proposal volatility). BTC $78,214. Next BOJ meeting June 15-16. Grades 1-2w B (forced unwind signatures present); 3-4w C (fragility persists, but stabilization possible if yen holds and geopolitics cool). Watch MOF follow-on action, USDJPY defense of 155-157, and JGB yield behavior.

stack.note: Plunged ~3% May 1 (160.66→155.5 intraday) on suspected MOF intervention; closed ~157; 5d -2.8%; pace trigger hit.
US 10Y 4.39%, JGB 10Y 2.51% (1997 high); spread compressing as JGB sells off on fiscal/inflation fears despite BOJ hold.
Highest since 1997; oil-driven inflation fears + fiscal stress; three BOJ members dissented for hike Apr 28.
Down modestly as oil eased; Iran sent proposal May 1, crude retreated but remains >$100; Fed neutral.
Below 100; rates vol subsiding after intervention spike, but JGB stress keeps fixed-income volatility live.
Subdued; equity vol disconnected from FX stress; S&P hit records May 1 despite yen chaos—complacency flag.
Dropped after Iran sent peace proposal May 1; remains elevated (Hormuz closed); ¥/bbl = ~15,900 (import inflation pressure).
Channel breakout confirmed; institutional flows strong; macro rotation into risk assets despite carry unwind—divergence or lead?
Modest gain despite 3% yen surge—exporters supported but financials under pressure; JGB stress and carry unwind weigh on banks.

trigger_map.note: Fired May 1: Katayama 'final warning' morning, USDJPY plunged 160→155.5; BOJ data implies ~$35B spent (unconfirmed). Follow-on rounds likely if yen weakens.
BOJ held 0.75% Apr 28 (6-3 vote; three dissented for 1%); raised CPI to 2.8%, cut growth to 0.5%; next meeting June 15-16—hike risk elevated.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: 2.51% = highest since 1997; oil inflation + fiscal stress + BOJ tightening path driving sell-off. 2.60% next key level (1996 peak).
MOVE at 70.4, down from recent highs; rates vol contained but JGB instability keeps fixed-income stress simmering.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: WTI $101 (down from $111 high); Iran proposal May 1 cooled spike, but Hormuz closed and Trump maintains blockade—¥/bbl ~15,900 elevated.
FX basis data unavailable; intervention mechanics (BOJ draining ¥) suggest temporary tightening, but no systemic USD funding alarm yet.

narrative.p1: MOF intervened May 1 after USDJPY broke 160.66 (highest since July 2024). Finance Minister Katayama issued a 'final warning' that morning; by afternoon, USDJPY plunged to 155.5 intraday (~3% spike), closing ~157. BOJ current-account data suggests ~$35B spent (net outflow 9.48T yen vs. 4-4.5T forecast). BOJ had held rates at 0.75% Apr 28 but raised FY2026 core CPI to 2.8% (from 1.9%) and cut growth to 0.5% (from 1%), citing Iran war terms-of-trade shock. Three of nine board members dissented for hike to 1%—most hawkish signal in years. JGB 10Y hit 2.51% (1997 high) on inflation fears; Nikkei +0.4% despite yen surge. WTI dropped to $101 after Iran sent updated peace proposal, but Hormuz remains closed. VIX 16.99 (equities calm), MOVE 70.4 (rates vol easing). Stage shift: 1 → 3 on intervention trigger and sharp yen move.
narrative.p2: US-JP 10Y spread 187 bps (down 20 from ~207 pre-intervention), compressing as JGB sells off faster than UST. Carry-to-vol proxy degraded: 187 bps spread over elevated (but unmeasured) USDJPY vol = carry less attractive. USDJPY 5-day pace: -2.8% (intervention-driven, but MOF historically reacts to pace >1.5%/week—threshold met). Distance from 60d mean: USDJPY at 157 vs. ~158.5 mean = near neutral post-spike, but 160.66 high was deeply stretched. BOJ policy rate 0.75%, but three dissenters + raised inflation forecast = next hike (likely June or July) priced at ~50% by markets. JGB 10Y at 1997 highs signals fiscal/inflation stress; curve steepening (long-end selling) is classic pre-hike positioning. Stage 3 justified: MOF fired, yen spiked, vol jumped, carry liquidating. Grades 1-2w B: intervention likely serial (historical pattern), unwind momentum building, cross-asset spillover risk (JGB → global rates, yen → EM carry). Grades 3-4w C: if USDJPY holds below 160 and oil cools further, carry may stabilize; however, BOJ June meeting and JGB instability sustain fragility. **Applied unwind prior for 3w/4w: data genuinely ambiguous (Iran proposal vs. BOJ hike vs. intervention success)—leaned one notch more alert (would otherwise grade 3w/4w D)**.
narrative.p3: BTC $78,214 (+1.4% overnight, +2.4% on May 1), breaking out of multi-month descending channel. Institutional ETF inflows strong in April ($2.6B), though outflows resumed late month (-$490M Apr 27-29). BTC-DXY correlation: DXY down -0.1% while BTC rallies—decoupling suggests crypto rotating independent of USD carry dynamics, possibly front-running risk-on if Iran deal progresses. XRP data unavailable, but BTC open interest rising (exchange reserves falling) = supply shock + demand = bullish setup. Crypto sympathy read: if MOF intervention + BOJ hike compress yen carry, levered EM/Asia flows could rotate into crypto as alternative yield—BTC acting as 'digital gold' plus tech beta. Risk: if Stage 4 forced unwind cascades (equity selloff, HY widening), BTC historically correlates ~0.6-0.7 with SPX during vol spikes—current divergence (SPX records, BTC rallying, VIX 17) is either early rotation signal or mispricing. Watch BTC $80K breakout vs. USDJPY 160 defense; if both hold, crypto positioned well. If USDJPY re-breaks 160 and VIX spikes, BTC likely pulls back with risk-off.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: MOF intervention May 1 was likely first of serial rounds (2024 precedent: two interventions, $60B total). USDJPY at 157 is above July 2024 intervention low (~154), so re-test of 160 possible if USD strength returns. BOJ next meets June 15-16, but markets will front-run hike expectations. JGB 10Y at 2.51% = fiscal stress + inflation pass-through from oil—if WTI rebounds above $110, JGB could spike to 2.60%+ and force BOJ emergency action. Grades both weeks B: forced unwind signatures present (carry deleveraging, vol elevated, policy tightening priced), and MOF follow-on intervention risk high if yen weakens. VIX complacency (16.99 vs. MOVE 70, JGB chaos) is fragile—equity vol could catch up if risk-off accelerates.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 ends ~May 16, week 3 ~May 23. BOJ meeting June 15-16 sits just outside 3-week window but will dominate positioning by late May. If USDJPY stabilizes 155-157 and oil continues to ease (Iran proposal progresses), carry pressure may plateau—hence grade shift B → C at 3w. However, JGB stress and BOJ hike probability sustain tail risk: if JGB 10Y breaks 2.60%, curve steepening could force pension/bank deleveraging, widening credit spreads and triggering Stage 4 contagion. Watch for: (1) MOF verbal intervention frequency (daily = cocked for more action), (2) USDJPY 160 re-test, (3) HY spread widening (HYG/JNK data unavailable, but credit sympathy to JGB stress). Applied unwind prior for 3w: data ambiguous (intervention success vs. BOJ hike vs. geopolitics), leaned C over D.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 4 ends ~May 30. If MOF intervention holds and Iran ceasefire firms, USDJPY could drift back toward 158-159 without re-breaking 160—carry partially rebuilds, grades C sustained. However, BOJ June meeting looms (1 week beyond 4w horizon), and markets will price hike by end-May if inflation data (Tokyo CPI May 23, National CPI May 30) prints hot. JGB 10Y behavior critical: if yield stabilizes <2.55%, worst may be priced; if >2.60%, fiscal crisis narrative accelerates. Grade 4w C: fragility persists (BOJ path, JGB stress, oil tail risk), but acute forced unwind may be past if intervention succeeded and yen holds. Applied unwind prior for 4w: ambiguous whether June BOJ hike is bullish (restores confidence) or bearish (confirms inflation crisis)—leaned C over D to keep BOJ hike risk visible.

watch_list: MOF follow-on intervention: if USDJPY re-approaches 160, Katayama/Ueda verbal or actual intervention likely—serial pattern from 2024.
BOJ June 15-16 meeting pricing: if JGB 10Y breaks 2.60% or Tokyo CPI (May 23) surprises high, hike probability jumps to >70%—yen rallies, carry unwinds further.
Iran ceasefire progress: Trump maintains blockade, but Iran proposal May 1 cooled oil—if deal firms and WTI falls <$95, ¥ import inflation eases and BOJ hike urgency drops.
USDJPY defense of 155-157 zone: if pair holds below 160 for 5+ days, carry deleveraging may stabilize; break back above 160 = MOF credibility test and likely re-intervention.
Cross-asset vol spillover: VIX 17 vs. MOVE 70 and JGB chaos = disconnect—watch for equity vol catch-up (VIX >20) if JGB selling triggers margin calls or credit widening.
